commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=20bd59e9d1f7e00d8baac70aecb8cce89b79d381)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Signal native/text mention facts to generic inbound message context, docs, and Plugin SDK runtime mock parity.

PR surface: Source +112, Tests +247, Docs +28. Total +387 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect at source level: PR head computes `MentionShouldSkip` from the mention decision, but Signal returns before building generic inbound context for skipped turns. The central PR capability is a new context surface rather than a failing current-main bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the public `MsgContext` shape and stack ordering, and the contributor needs to add real setup proof; this is not a safe automated repair lane as-is.

target.title: fix(signal): expose mention facts in inbound context
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal group mention gating now has native mention metadata available, but the decision is only visible inside the Signal monitor. Downstream prompt/tool context cannot inspect whether the inbound group message had native mention metadata, whether `requireMention` was enforced, or why a message was skipped. That makes native-only group behavior hard to debug and forces consumers to infer channel-specific facts from text.

## Summary

- Exposes Signal mention-gating facts on the generic inbound message context.
- Adds `CanDetectMention`, `HasAnyMention`, `RequireMention`, `EffectiveWasMentioned`, `MentionShouldSkip`, and `MentionSource` for native/text mention decisions.
- Keeps the Signal mention source literal typed so SDK/context consumers can rely on `"native"` vs `"mention_pattern"`.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738 (`fix(signal): detect native bot mentions in group gating`). Until #96738 lands, this PR's diff will include that dependency.

## Production impact

No new outbound behavior. This only makes already-computed mention gating facts inspectable by prompts, tests, and plugin/channel contracts.

## Evidence

- Focused Signal/channel tests cover native mention facts being projected into inbound context:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Plugin SDK runtime mock tests cover `Mention*` parity with production inbound context.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Focused SDK mock test passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production `buildChannelInboundEventContext` and printed the emitted prompt context on PR head `20bd59e9d1f7`.

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<redacted proof harness>'
runtime-proof:environment
{
  "pr": 96862,
  "repo": "openclaw/openclaw",
  "head": "20bd59e9d1f7",
  "node": "v26.3.0"
}
runtime-proof:#96862 native mention facts emitted by prompt context
[
  {
    "label": "native bot mention",
    "MentionSource": "native",
    "CanDetectMention": true,
    "HasAnyMention": true,
    "WasMentioned": true,
    "ExplicitlyMentionedBot": true,
    "RequireMention": true,
    "EffectiveWasMentioned": true,
    "MentionShouldSkip": false,
    "MentionedUserIds": [
      "signal:<redacted-bot-uuid>"
    ]
  },
  {
    "label": "native mention of another participant",
    "MentionSource": "none",
    "CanDetectMention": true,
    "HasAnyMention": true,
    "WasMentioned": false,
    "ExplicitlyMentionedBot": false,
    "RequireMention": true,
    "EffectiveWasMentioned": false,
    "MentionShouldSkip": true,
    "MentionedUserIds": [
      "signal:<redacted-other-participant-uuid>"
    ]
  }
]
```

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
target.author: pdurlej

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
